name: Pico del Nevero and Lagunillas Loop from Puerto de Navafría
name_original: Subida al Pico del Nevero por las Lagunilla del Nevero — circular por el Puerto de Navafría - Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama
description: This difficult 4.7-mile jogging route to Pico del Nevero offers panoramic views and passes glacial lagoons in Sierra de Guadarrama.
region_intro: Jogging around Aldealengua De Pedraza offers a variety of routes through a landscape characterized by mountain passes, elevated viewpoints, and open terrain. The region features a mix of challenging ascents to peaks and more moderate paths around reservoirs. Runners can expect diverse scenery, including expansive views and varied natural surfaces. Best jogging routes around Aldealengua De Pedraza The most popular jogging route is Embalse de Pinilla – running circular from Lozoya, a 9.9 miles (15.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 44 minutes to complete. This circular route offers views of the reservoir and surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local runners is Pico del Nevero and Lagunillas Loop from Puerto de Navafría, a difficult 4.7 miles (7.5 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain, leading through mountain passes and offering views of peaks. Local runners also love the Views of Pedraza loop from Pedraza, a 8.1 miles (13.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views of the historic town, often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes. Jogging around Aldealengua De Pedraza is defined by mountain passes, elevated viewpoints, and reservoir-side paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Aldealengua De Pedraza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 160 runners have used komoot to explore Aldealengua De Pedraza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Aldealengua De Pedraza?
A: There are over 30 dedicated running routes around Aldealengua De Pedraza, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These include easy paths, moderate trails, and challenging mountain runs.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Aldealengua De Pedraza?
A: Yes, Aldealengua De Pedraza offers a few easier running options. While many routes involve significant elevation, there are at least 2 routes classified as easy, and 16 as moderate, which can be suitable for beginners looking for less strenuous runs. These often follow more gentle terrain or reservoir-side paths.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running trails around Aldealengua De Pedraza are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958007928/embalse-de-pinilla-running-circular-from-lozoya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Pinilla – running circular from Lozoya&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 9.9-mile loop offering scenic views.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the jogging trails?
A: The running routes in Aldealengua De Pedraza showcase diverse scenery, from expansive views across open terrain to paths alongside reservoirs and through mountain passes. You'll encounter varied natural surfaces and enjoy the region's characteristic landscape, including elevated viewpoints.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or landmarks I can see while running?
A: Yes, several routes pass by notable attractions. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1164666887/pico-del-nevero-and-lagunillas-loop-from-puerto-de-navafria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Nevero and Lagunillas Loop from Puerto de Navafría&lt;/a&gt; offers views of peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4078211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nevero Peak&lt;/a&gt;. Other highlights in the broader area include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5148338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinilla Reservoir Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and various mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4003184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navafría Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Aldealengua De Pedraza?
A: The running routes in Aldealengua De Pedraza are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging ascents, and the rewarding views from elevated points and mountain passes.

Q: Are there any running routes suitable for families?
A: While many routes feature significant elevation, the 2 easy and 16 moderate routes can be suitable for families looking for less demanding runs. Consider routes with gentler gradients or those around reservoirs for a more family-friendly experience. Always check the route details for distance and elevation before heading out.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many trails in natural areas around Aldealengua De Pedraza, especially if kept on a leash. However, it's always best to check local regulations or specific route details, particularly in protected areas or near livestock, to ensure a pleasant and safe experience for everyone.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Aldealengua De Pedraza?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for jogging in Aldealengua De Pedraza, with milder temperatures and beautiful natural colors. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, so early mornings or late evenings are preferable. Winter running is possible, particularly on lower paths, but higher mountain routes may require appropriate gear for snow and ice.

Q: Are there any routes that offer good opportunities for wildlife spotting?
A: Given the region's natural landscape of mountain passes and open terrain, many routes offer opportunities for wildlife spotting, especially during quieter times of day. Keep an eye out for local bird species and other small animals, particularly on trails that venture away from populated areas and into more secluded natural environments.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails?
A: Many popular trailheads, especially those near mountain passes like Puerto de Navafría, typically have designated parking areas. For routes starting from towns or villages, street parking might be available. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for specific parking information.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of running routes in Aldealengua De Pedraza?
A: The routes around Aldealengua De Pedraza offer a mix of difficulties. You'll find 2 easy routes, 16 moderate routes, and 12 difficult routes. The difficult trails often involve significant elevation gain and challenging terrain, making them suitable for experienced runners seeking a demanding workout.
tips: The trail leaves the pine forest and continues up the mountain range to the cliffs of Alto del Puerto. The hard slopes are over. The path markings continue through the rope meadows towards the main summit of the massif. They first pass through the valley formed by the pass where the Hornillo stream is born and continue to the block at the geodesic vertex of Nevero (2,209 m). In the surroundings of the summit there are remains of old constructions and old stone walls. The views are spectacular. On a clear, cloudless day, the Lozoya Valley is beautiful, surrounded by the reliefs of Cuerda Larga, the Altos de Hontanares and forests on all the slopes. The chimneys of the towns always release some smoke to enrich the mountain landscape with rural life. And at the head of the valley, the Peñalara massif stands out on the isolated and energetic peak line, it is the great sentinel of the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Just below the Pico del Nevero, where huge rocks that descend to the bottom of it have given birth to the Lagunilla del Nevero, of glacial origin. It is worth taking a short break to see its clear waters.
On the list of the ''first'' riders in Madrid, Navafría is the one most enjoyed by the cyclist on foot. It's a cool, shady, twisty, tough, quiet route.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult, especially if attempting it as a jogging trail as suggested. The terrain includes steep ascents, particularly a challenging 4 km stretch towards Nevero Peak, and requires sure-footedness. It is generally better suited for experienced hiking or trail running rather than casual jogging.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied terrain, starting from the recreational area of Las Lagunillas near Navafría Pass. You'll encounter forest tracks, narrower paths, and short sections of road. Expect steep ascents, rocky slopes (canchales), and potentially indistinct paths due to fallen branches. Some parts might be wide, while others narrow significantly as you gain elevation.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The circular route typically starts from the recreational area of Las Lagunillas, which is located near Puerto de Navafría. Parking is generally available in this area, serving as a convenient trailhead for the route.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to visit this trail?
faqs.answer: The best times to visit are typically spring, summer, and autumn when conditions are milder. In winter, snow and ice are common, making access and passage difficult and potentially requiring specialized equipment like snowshoes or crampons. Always check local weather and trail conditions before heading out.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this trail in Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama?
faqs.answer: As this trail is located within the Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama, specific regulations apply to dogs. Generally, dogs are permitted but must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always check the latest park regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or specific permits required for general access to hiking trails within the Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama, including this route. However, always respect park regulations and leave no trace.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This route offers spectacular scenery. You'll traverse through dense Scots pine forests, pass the impressive Cascada del Chorro waterfall, and reach the Lagoons of Pico del Nevero, which are small glacial lagoons. From the summit of Nevero Peak, you'll enjoy breathtaking panoramic views of the Lozoya Valley, Cuerda Larga, Peñalara massif, and the Segovian plateau. You might also spot historical elements like trenches and bunkers from the Spanish Civil War near the summit.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the [CIMA MA06] Navafría, La Horizontal, [CIMA SE03] Navafría, and Pista esquí de fondo Navafría during your journey.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for this challenging route?
faqs.answer: Given the difficult rating and varied terrain, wear sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support. Layered clothing is recommended, as weather conditions can change quickly at higher altitudes. Bring plenty of water, snacks, a map or GPS device, sun protection, and a first-aid kit. If attempting in winter, specialized equipment like snowshoes or crampons may be necessary.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or accommodation options near Puerto de Navafría?
faqs.answer: While direct options at Puerto de Navafría might be limited, you can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation in nearby towns such as Rascafría and Lozoya. These towns are popular bases for exploring the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the trailhead from Madrid?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is possible to reach the general area by public transport from Madrid. Buses typically run from Madrid to towns like Rascafría or Lozoya. From there, you might need to arrange local transport or a taxi to reach the trailhead at Las Lagunillas near Puerto de Navafría, as direct bus services to the pass itself can be infrequent.
